verdict_reasoning: KLK2 (kallikrein-2) is a secreted serine protease synthesized as a pre-pro-enzyme, cleaved in the secretory pathway, and released into prostatic fluid and serum. It has a signal peptide but no TM domain, no GPI anchor, no outer-leaflet lipidation. Walking contextual buckets: (1) cell_state_induced — no documented cancer/cell-death/activation ecto-pool of the protein body; (2) tissue_restricted_surface — prostate-restricted expression but as a secreted enzyme, not surface-anchored; (3) lysosomal_exocytosis — not a lysosomal TM protein; (4) dual_localization — no documented stable PM minority pool; (5) stable_surface_attachment — no wash-resistant covalent/non-covalent tethering to a TM partner documented. Clinical KLK2-targeting programs (e.g., JNJ-78278343 CD3 bispecific, PSMA-like radioligand efforts) target the secreted/locally-deposited pool, not a stable cell-surface form.
predicted_key_uncertainty: Some reports suggest KLK2 may associate with prostate cancer cell surfaces via protease-inhibitor or substrate interactions, but wash-resistant tethering is not clearly established.
